reasoning_model_1: The model completely failed the 'Content Accuracy' criterion. Instead of reading the specified sentence ('The professor's question was so sudden, I need to quickly think of an answer.'), the model hallucinated a completely different, extended script ('...wham so sudden, I gotta think fast, no time to breathe... just go go go'). While the delivery of this hallucinated text was indeed rushed, the total deviation from the requested script constitutes a critical failure. According to the evaluation standards, if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score must be 0.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the script perfectly with 100% content accuracy. Regarding the 'Rushed Rhythm' feature, the delivery is excellent. From 00:00 to 00:01 ('The professor's question was so sudden'), the speaker adopts a tone of immediate urgency. From 00:01 to 00:03 ('I need to quickly think of an answer'), the pace noticeably accelerates, and the articulation becomes tighter and faster, effectively simulating the internal panic and rushed thought process described in the text. The prosody is natural and the emotional coloring of anxiety/urgency enhances the perception of the rushed rhythm.
model_comparison: Model 1 failed the fundamental task of reading the provided text, generating a hallucinated monologue instead. This results in an automatic failure regardless of the paralinguistic style. Model 2 accurately read the text and successfully implemented the 'rushed rhythm' by increasing the speaking rate and injecting a sense of urgency and anxiety into the tone, particularly in the second half of the sentence. Model 2 is the clear winner.
